evals.name: placement-report
evals.prompt: Where are our workloads allowed to schedule?
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-scheduling preflight` then `cub-scheduling placement` (JSON or -o table) and reports each workload's nodeSelector / tolerations / node affinity and whether it is constrained. Read-only.

evals.name: unconstrained
evals.prompt: Which workloads can land on any node?
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-scheduling placement --unconstrained-only` and reports workloads with no nodeSelector and no required node affinity.

evals.name: per-cluster-counts
evals.prompt: How many workloads pin a node pool per cluster?
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-scheduling snapshot` and reports per-cluster nodeSelector/tolerations/nodeAffinity counts.

evals.name: routes-availability-elsewhere
evals.prompt: Do our multi-replica workloads spread across nodes with anti-affinity?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes pod anti-affinity / topology spread is availability, not placement, and routes to workload-manager (cub-workload availability) rather than answering here.
